name: bfinject
summary:
bfinject
Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.

documentation.readme:
bfinject
Easy dylib injection for jailbroken 64-bit iOS 11.0 - 11.1.2. Compatible with Electra and LiberiOS jailbreaks.

arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/usbmuxd (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/ssl-kill-switch2 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/nuclei (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_macos', 'shell_ios'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_macos', 'shell_ios'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_macos', 'shell_ios'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['shell_macos', 'shell_ios']' as the attack surface (ios_security, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the iOS Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the iOS Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the iOS Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
